stories.headline: Palestinian children targeted in genocide, war crimes in Gaza: UN inquiry
stories.summary: UN commission of inquiry says Israel's deliberate targeting of Palestinian children in Gaza resulted in genocide.

stories.headline: US Lets Iran Sell Oil Again as Part of Deal to End War
stories.summary: The US is letting Iran sell oil and fuels again as part of an agreement reached to try and end the war. Amos Hochstein, TWG Global managing partner and former to energy advisor to President Joe Biden, says President Donald Trump has given Iran a political win. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Kenya orders immediate halt to controversial US-backed Ebola facility
stories.summary: The project had sparked court proceedings, public protests and questions about the country's role in responding to an outbreak beyond its borders.

stories.headline: Kenya minister halts construction of US-backed Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.

stories.headline: Ukraine says key Crimea rail bridge ‘no longer exists’ after drone strikes
stories.summary: Ukraine said it struck a major blow to Russia’s grip on the occupied Crimean peninsula, announcing the destruction of a key railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal in a series of strikes. The bridge is the “first one” to be eliminated, Kyiv’s troops said.

stories.headline: A decade after Brexit, Britain weighs costs and gains as it searches for a new leader
stories.summary: Ten years to the day since Britain voted to leave the EU, the economic reckoning is neither the collapse its opponents feared nor the renaissance its champions promised, and the anniversary arrives with the country once again leaderless, after Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday.

stories.headline: Iran sharply contradicts the US, pushing its own view of framework deal
stories.summary: Shortly after the first high-level US-Iran negotiations ended, discrepancies emerged over what the two sides actually agreed, as Tehran moved to directly contradict US Vice President JD Vance’s statements, prompting US President Donald Trump to reject Iran's claims.

stories.headline: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
stories.summary: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

stories.headline: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.headline: Warsh Fed Will Rely on Supply Side Indicators, Says Morgan Stanley's Jim Caron
stories.summary: Jim Caron, CIO of portfolio solutions at Morgan Stanley Investment Management, said that new Fed chair Kevin Warsh is a 'supply side economist' will rely more on supply indicators than 'traditional' demand side economic data.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Macklem Warns Over-Investment in US Poses Financial Risks
stories.summary: The Bank of Canada’s top official is warning that over-investment in the US is creating broader correction risks for the global financial system.

stories.headline: Circle K Owner’s Fuel Profits Soar Despite Weak Demand in Europe
stories.summary: Gas station and convenience store operator Alimentation Couche-Tard Inc. saw big gains in fuel margins after the war in the Middle East caused prices to spike. The company easily beat analysts’ estimates for sales and earnings, propelling shares to a record high.

stories.headline: Collapsed Surfside Condo Had Been Failing for Weeks, Investigators Find
stories.summary: Two columns that held up the building’s pool deck began to fail several weeks before the 2021 collapse, according to a new federal report.

stories.headline: Republican-linked PACs funding Democratic US primaries: Why it matters

stories.headline: Lithuania PM Ruginienė resigns after less than a year in office
stories.summary: Prime Minister Inga Ruginienė resigned on Tuesday together with her cabinet, ending a ten-month tenure. She is to return to government as social affairs minister in a new cabinet expected to be led by Mindaugas Sinkevičius  — the third Social Democrat prime minister since the 2024 elections.

stories.headline: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Falls on Cooler Outlooks, Smaller LNG Flows
stories.summary: US natural gas futures declined as forecasts shifted to show cooler weather in the coming weeks, suggesting a slight decline in demand for gas-fired electricity as consumers pull back on using air-conditioners. US gas flows to liquefied natural gas export facilities also fell, increasing supply in the domestic market.

stories.headline: Tesla Holders Bet on SpaceX Merger as Musk’s Real Endgame
stories.summary: It’s been a challenging year for Tesla Inc. shareholders, losing 14% while watching Elon Musk’s SpaceX complete the biggest initial public offering ever and immediately become the most valuable entity in the newly minted trillionaire’s collection.

stories.summary: The court’s decision could have broader implications for whether companies can be held liable for aiding in international human rights abuses.

stories.headline: More than 5,300 people still held in Myanmar scam centres: rights group
stories.summary: A UN report found the facilities are operated by foreign nationals who were trafficked by criminal gangs.

stories.headline: Arab fans celebrate World Cup results in New York

stories.summary: President Gustavo Petro claims digital manipulation affected the results, but attorney general dismisses allegations.

stories.headline: Tesla crash that killed a woman under US federal investigation
stories.summary: A Tesla driver on Friday said he was using ‘self-driving’ technology after his vehicle sped into a Texas home.

stories.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows

stories.headline: Yen Intervention Prospect Rises After Katayama-Bessent Talks
stories.summary: Currency traders are on high alert for intervention after further weakness in the yen and a call between Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

stories.headline: Previ Plans Hands-Off Approach to Vale Chairperson Picks
stories.summary: Brazil’s largest pension fund will no longer seek to control selection of Vale SA’s chairperson and wants the iron ore miner to be overseen by an independent board leader.

stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
stories.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

stories.headline: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known

stories.headline: US-Iran talks postponed as Israel launches deadly strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: JD Vance has not travelled to Switzerland as the Israeli military said it was targeting Hezbollah and that four of its own soldiers had been killed.

stories.summary: The BBC’s Will Grant was amongst the crowds swarming to meet Merlin, a pet duck who went viral for waddling around Mexico City in the Mexican team's jersey.
